name: Botswana

visa_free.notes: No visa required. Bilateral visa exemption for nationals signed September 8, 2022.
Hong Kong SAR passport holders listed as visa-free on official Embassy page.
No visa required. Namibian citizens may also use national identity cards following a bilateral agreement effective February 2023.

Listed as visa-free on the official Botswana Embassy Washington DC page (ordinary passport). Wikipedia's Visa policy of Botswana notes '30 days each visit, but 90 days within any 365-day period' for Russia - this per-visit limit is not reflected on the Embassy page; official Embassy source records 90 days.
Listed as 'San Marico' (typo) on official Embassy page; interpreted as San Marino.
Listed as 'South Korea (Republic of)' on official Embassy page.
Listed as 'Swaziland' on official Embassy page; current name is Eswatini.

Listed as visa-free on the official Botswana Embassy Washington DC page. Added during June 2026 validation - was MISSING from prior record. No official bilateral agreement date found on gov.bw, but the Embassy page (official source) explicitly includes Turkey in the visa-free list.
No visa required. Embassy page specifically highlights US citizens as not requiring a visa.

e_visa.nationality: Countries required to obtain a visa (as listed on evisa.gov.bw - full list)
e_visa.notes: Citizens of the following countries require a visa and may apply online via evisa.gov.bw. Countries listed: Aaland Islands, Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, American Samoa, Andorra, Anguilla, Armenia, Aruba, Ascension Islands, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Belarus, Benin, Bermuda, Bhutan, Bolivia, Bonaire Sint Eustatius and Saba, Bosnia Herzegovina, Bouvet Island, British Virgin Islands, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cambodia, Cameroon, Cape Verde Islands, Cayman Islands, Central African Rep, Chad, China, Christmas Island, Colombia, Comoros and Mayotte Islands, Congo, Congo DRC, Cook Islands, Cote d'Ivoire, Curacao, Djibouti, East Timor, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Faeroe Islands, Falkland Islands, Fiji Islands, French Antilles, French Guiana, French Polynesia, French Southern Territories, Gabon, Georgia, Ghana, Gibraltar, Greenland, Guadeloupe, Guam, Guatemala, Guinea, Guinea Bissau, Haiti, Heard and McDonald Island, Honduras, India, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Lebanon, Liberia, Libya, Macau (PRC), Macedonia, Madagascar, Mali, Marshall Island, Martinique, Mauritania, Mayotte, Micronesia, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Montserrat, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Netherlands Antilles, New Caledonia, Nicaragua, Niger, Nigeria, Niue Island, Norfolk Island, North Korea, Northern Mariana Islands, Pakistan, Palau, Palestine, Panama, Philippines, Pitcairn, Puerto Rico, Reunion Islands, Rwanda, Saint Barthelemy, Saint Martin French part, Sao Tome, Senegal, Serbia, Sint Maarten Dutch part, Somalia, South Georgia, Sri Lanka, St. Helena, St. Pierre and Miquelon, Sudan, Suriname, Svalbard and Ljan Mayen Islands, Syria, Taiwan (PRC), Tajikistan, Thailand, Togo, Tokelau, Tunisia, Turkmenistan, Turks and Caicos Islands, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Vietnam, Virgin Islands British, Virgin Islands US, Wallis and Futuna, Western Sahara, Western Samoa, Yemen. Visa fees per botswanaembassy.org: Tourist single entry (up to 1 month) US$30; multiple entry (up to 3 months) US$50. Visitor single entry (up to 3 months) US$30; multiple entry US$50.

visa_policy.notes: Botswana operates an e-Visa system at evisa.gov.bw for nationalities that require a visa. The official Botswana Embassy Washington DC page (botswanaembassy.org) provides the full list of visa-free countries. The evisa.gov.bw portal provides the list of countries requiring a visa. The 90-day max_stay is based on the general gov.bw statement and the Embassy page. NOTE: 'Yugoslavia' appears on the official embassy visa-free list but is a defunct state; not included in structured data. NOTE: The official Embassy page simultaneously lists 'Holy Sea' (typo for Holy See) in the visa-FREE section and 'Vatican City' in the visa-REQUIRED section - these refer to the same sovereign state (iso3: VAT). This is an unresolved official-source self-contradiction; Holy See/Vatican City is excluded from visa_free and flagged as a gap per the safety rule to classify down when in doubt. See data_quality.gaps.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official or service passports of China do not require a visa to enter Botswana for up to 30 days. Based on bilateral Agreement on Reciprocal Visa Waiver for Holders of Diplomatic, Official and Service Passports, signed 22 December 2018.

Diplomatic passports of India only. Ordinary and official/service passport holders from India still require a visa. Bilateral visa exemption agreement signed 01 November 2018, entered into force 31 January 2019.

cbi.program_name: Citizenship by Investment Programme (Citizenship Amendment Act 2025 / Impact Investment Program)

options.notes: The $75,000 minimum contribution figure is reported by Arton Capital (the MoU partner appointed in September 2025) via BotswanaCitizenship.com and widely cited in investment migration media. This figure has NOT been published in an official gov.bw gazette, regulation, or statutory instrument; it is noted here as the working figure pending official proclamation. The upper range cited is $90,000 under a limited quota system.

cbi.notes: The Citizenship (Amendment) Bill 2025 was passed by Parliament on December 17, 2025 (second reading), then sent to committee stage for clause-by-clause scrutiny. As of June 2026, no official gov.bw source confirms the bill has been signed into law or that regulations setting the investment threshold have been formally gazetted. President Duma Boko signed an MoU with Arton Capital on 26 September 2025 during the UN General Assembly, branding the program 'Impact Investment Program.' The $75,000–$90,000 minimum contribution figure originates from Arton Capital's registration portal (BotswanaCitizenship.com), not from a government gazette. Sectors targeted: manufacturing, mining and energy, agriculture, infrastructure, financial services, tourism, technology and digitalisation, sport and creatives, health, green energy and renewable energy. Investors granted citizenship are restricted from: voting, holding public office, accessing free government education, applying for government funding, being allocated state/tribal land, or serving in disciplined forces.

programs.name: Permanent Residence - Investor Category
programs.notes: Investors may apply for Permanent Residence even if they have stayed for less than five years (unlike other categories which require 5+ years continuous lawful residence). Required documents include: certified share valuations, share certificates, certificate of incorporation, list of directors, current financial statements, title deed/lease of business premises, trading license, and BURS tax return. Fee: P3,000. Processing time: 56 working days. No minimum investment amount is stated on the official page. The gov.bw sub-page continues to return HTTP 500 errors and could not be re-scraped during June 2026 validation; details preserved from prior verified research.

programs.name: Permanent Residence - 5-Year Residence
programs.notes: Non-citizen must have resided lawfully in Botswana continuously for at least 5 years, be of good character, have made significant contributions to Botswana, be of good standing in society, and have sufficient means of support. Fee: P3,000. Processing time: 56 working days.

programs.name: Residence Permit (General)
programs.notes: Any non-citizen wishing to stay in Botswana beyond the visa/visitation period can apply for a residence permit at any immigration office. Categories include dependants (18-25 years and 65+), immigrants, spouse of a citizen. Processing time: 30 working days. Fees: P1,500 for dependants. For immigrants: Form 15, Form 3, covering letter, certified passport, certified birth certificate, bank statement, proof of maintenance. The gov.bw sub-page returned HTTP 500 during June 2026 re-validation; details preserved from prior verified research.

programs.name: Work Permit
programs.eligibility: Non-citizens who wish to work in Botswana for reward, or invest in Botswana for profit. Requires waiver from Department of Labour and Social Security, or copies of work and residence permits, confirmation of employment, and relevant qualifications.
programs.notes: Official gov.bw page describes a Work Permit giving non-citizens permission to work in Botswana for reward or to invest for profit. A Point-Based System (PBS) for work permits is referenced in official Botswana Government communications, but a dedicated accessible official web page with full PBS details was not available. The gov.bw sub-page returned HTTP 500 during June 2026 re-validation.

agreements: SADC (Southern African Development Community) - founding member since 1980; has ratified the SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons (2005), but the protocol has not yet entered into force regionally due to insufficient ratifications by all member states
SACU (Southern African Customs Union) - member; facilitates trade but does not establish freedom of movement for ordinary citizens
Botswana-Namibia bilateral identity card agreement (February 2023) - Namibian nationals may enter Botswana using national identity cards in lieu of passports

validation_notes: CORRECTION (June 2026, initial run): Turkey (TUR) was MISSING from visa_free in the prior record. The official Botswana Embassy Washington DC page explicitly lists 'Turkey' in the visa-free country list. Turkey has been added to visa_policy.visa_free.

CORRECTION (June 2026, initial run): CBI options[] updated to include the $75,000 minimum contribution figure cited by Arton Capital (MoU partner); note added clarifying this figure is NOT yet officially gazetted on gov.bw.
CONFIRMED (June 2026, initial run): Russia (RUS) is listed as visa-free for ordinary passports on the official Embassy page - existing record is correct.
CONFIRMED (June 2026, initial run): South Sudan (SSD) confirmed in visa-free list - existing record is correct.
ADDITION (June 2026, initial run): Namibia-Botswana ID card agreement (February 2023) added to agreements[] and Namibia entry notes.
ADDITION (June 2026, initial run): Brazil bilateral visa exemption agreement date (September 8, 2022) added to Brazil entry notes.
CONFIRMED (June 2026, initial run): India diplomatic passport conditional access and China diplomatic/official/service passport conditional access both confirmed correct - no changes.

CORRECTION (June 27, 2026, re-validation): Prior June 2026 validation note incorrectly stated 'Vatican City appearing in the visa-required section no longer observed on re-verification.' Fresh re-fetch of the Embassy page on June 27, 2026 confirms 'Vatican City' IS still listed in the visa-REQUIRED section AND 'Holy Sea' (typo for Holy See) IS simultaneously listed in the visa-FREE section. These are two separate entries on the same official page for what is legally the same sovereign entity (Holy See / Vatican City State, iso3: VAT). This is an unresolved self-contradiction on the official source. Per safety rules (classify down when in doubt), Holy See/Vatican City has been REMOVED from visa_free and moved to data_quality.gaps. The prior erroneous correction note has been superseded by this entry.
gaps: The 90-day max_stay for visa-free nationals is based on a general gov.bw statement and the Embassy page rather than a per-country table - individual bilateral agreements may set different limits. Russia Wikipedia source suggests '30 days per visit' limit not reflected on Embassy page.
The official Botswana Embassy Washington DC page lists 'Yugoslavia' as visa-free; Yugoslavia is a defunct state and was excluded from structured data.
No official government source (*.gov.bw) confirms a minimum investment amount for a dedicated investor residence permit or golden visa.
CBI $75,000 minimum investment figure comes from Arton Capital's BotswanaCitizenship.com portal, not from a government gazette; official regulations have not been published on gov.bw as of June 2026.
Citizenship by Investment (Citizenship Amendment Bill 2025) was passed by Parliament in December 2025 but status of presidential assent and formal enactment is unconfirmed on gov.bw as of June 2026.
Work Permit Point-Based System details referenced in official Botswana Government communications but no stable official web page was accessible during research.
The SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons has been ratified by Botswana but has not entered into force regionally.

Multiple gov.bw sub-pages (/residency-and-work/work-permit-application, /residency-and-work/permanent-residence-application, /visa-applications/visa-application-tourism-visa, /taxonomy/term/114) returned HTTP 500 errors during June 2026 re-validation and could not be scraped.

No official Botswana government source (*.gov.bw) found confirming credential-based conditional entry (i.e., entry for holders of US, UK, Schengen, Canadian, or other third-country visas or residence permits).
Turkey visa-free status confirmed from Embassy page, but no specific bilateral agreement date or treaty text found on gov.bw.

The evisa.gov.bw portal's JavaScript-rendered content was not accessible via WebFetch during June 2026 re-validation (page only returned navigation markup); the country list in e_visa[] is preserved from prior research based on the original evisa.gov.bw/#/needvisa scrape.

Holy See / Vatican City (iso3: VAT) - UNRESOLVED OFFICIAL-SOURCE SELF-CONTRADICTION: The official Botswana Embassy Washington DC page simultaneously lists 'Holy Sea' (typo for Holy See) in the visa-FREE section and 'Vatican City' in the visa-REQUIRED section. These are the same sovereign entity. Per safety rules, Holy See/Vatican City is excluded from visa_free pending resolution of this contradiction. If the 'Holy Sea' entry is the operative one, Holy See holders would be visa-free; if 'Vatican City' is operative, a visa is required. Botswana immigration authorities should be contacted directly to resolve this.

'Sumatra' appears in the visa-required list on the official Embassy page - Sumatra is an Indonesian island, not a country. This is an apparent data-entry error on the Embassy page; Indonesian passport holders are confirmed to require a visa (Indonesia is listed separately as visa-required). No structured entry was created for 'Sumatra'.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa - Single Entry
visa_types.purpose: Tourism activities in Botswana for up to one month. Requires a tour plan, hotel confirmations, proof of employment or business, and bank statement.
visa_types.notes: Processing is stated as 21 working days. Apply via evisa.gov.bw or at a Botswana embassy/consulate. Passport must have at least 6 months validity and 3 unused pages.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa - Multiple Entry
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry tourism visa for Botswana valid for up to three months. Requires tour plan, hotel confirmations, proof of employment or business, and bank statement.
visa_types.notes: Processing is stated as 21 working days. Apply via evisa.gov.bw or at a Botswana embassy/consulate.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Single Entry
visa_types.purpose: For visiting friends, family, or general visitation purposes for up to three months. Requires invitation letter from host, hotel bookings or residential address, host's affidavit, and financial documentation.
visa_types.notes: Processing is stated as 21 working days. Separate from tourist visa; intended for visiting hosts rather than general tourism.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Multiple Entry (up to 3 months)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry visitor visa for visiting friends or family in Botswana for up to three months. Requires invitation letter from host, hotel bookings or residential address, host's affidavit, and financial documentation.
visa_types.notes: Processing is stated as 21 working days.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa - Multiple Entry (Dependents, up to 2 years)
visa_types.purpose: Long-validity multiple-entry visitor visa for dependents visiting a resident in Botswana for over three months and up to two years.
visa_types.notes: Validity covers over 3 months up to 2 years. Intended for dependents of residents. Processing is stated as 21 working days.

visa_types.name: Business Visa - Single Entry
visa_types.purpose: For business meetings, establishing enterprises, or professional engagement in Botswana for up to three months. Requires business profile, meeting schedule, and host invitation.
visa_types.notes: Labour clearance required if staying 30 or more days. Processing is stated as 21 working days.

visa_types.name: Business Visa - Multiple Entry (up to 3 months)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry business visa for conducting business meetings, enterprise activities, or professional engagements in Botswana for up to three months.
visa_types.notes: Labour clearance required if staying 30 or more days. Processing is stated as 21 working days.

visa_types.name: Business Visa - Multiple Entry (6 months to 1 year)
visa_types.purpose: Long-validity multiple-entry business visa for ongoing business activities in Botswana for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Validity covers 6 months to 1 year. Labour clearance required if staying 30 or more days. Processing is stated as 21 working days.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals to work in Botswana under an employment contract. Requires a labour department waiver, employment contract, and relevant qualifications.
visa_types.notes: Fee not separately listed in the standard visa fee schedule on the Embassy page. A separate Work Permit is also required from the Department of Labour and Social Security (gov.bw). Processing is stated as 21 working days for the visa stage.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa - Multiple Entry (up to 6 months)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals investing in Botswana, allowing multiple entries for up to six months. Requires certificate of incorporation, trade license, and financial documentation.
visa_types.notes: Processing is stated as 21 working days. Investors may separately apply for Permanent Residence - Investor Category via gov.bw.

visa_types.name: Investor Visa - Multiple Entry (up to 5 years)
visa_types.purpose: Long-validity multiple-entry visa for established foreign investors in Botswana, valid for up to five years. Requires certificate of incorporation, trade license, share valuations, and financial documentation.
visa_types.notes: Processing is stated as 21 working days. The 5-year investor visa is the longest validity category listed on the Embassy fee schedule.

visa_types.name: Botswana Blue Card
visa_types.purpose: Grants former Botswana citizens who have renounced their citizenship the right to unlimited stay in Botswana, including visitation, residency, and work privileges.
visa_types.notes: Eligibility is restricted to former Botswana citizens (those who renounced citizenship after being born in or descended from Botswana). Spouses and children born after renunciation are not eligible unless they independently qualify. Blue Card holders cannot obtain an Omang (national ID) or Botswana passport. The $300 fee is non-refundable. Required documents: completed application form, certified passport copy, renunciation certificate, acquired citizenship proof, two passport photos.

outbound_official.note: no official Botswana government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.description: Official Botswana e-visa portal - home page (JS-rendered, not fully accessible via WebFetch in June 2026 validation)

Official Embassy of Botswana (Washington DC) - full visa-free and visa-required country lists, visa fees, diplomatic passport fee waiver. Re-fetched and verified June 27, 2026. CORRECTION: Prior note claiming Vatican City no longer in visa-required was WRONG - Vatican City still appears in visa-required. Holy Sea (visa-free) and Vatican City (visa-required) both present simultaneously on same page.
